case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for the adsorbate with SMILES [N]=N on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: OCD id=192; adsorbate=[N]=N; surface_formula=Si8Ta16V24; y_relaxed=-2.085077 eV; site=5;7;23;25;27

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=N, i.e. diazenyl/dinitrogen-like N2 fragment with two heavy atoms. RDKit reports atom 0 as N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; atom 1 as N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. There is no mismatch between requested adsorbate and SMILES, so I proceed based on the SMILES. Because one selected binding atom can legitimately be the radical center (atom 0), using ReactiveSpecies is chemically valid if I bind through atom 0. History analysis: only one successful plan exists so far, hollow on V-V-V with adsorbate bound at hollow and it relaxed to a hollow HCP/subsurface-related geometry, giving the current best energy of -6.585 eV. No [🔄 Converged to known best] tag is present, so I should not terminate. Also, only one site family/environment has been tested; major high-symmetry alternatives remain unexplored. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for [N]=N on this heterogeneous V/Ta/Si surface. Adsorbate analysis: a diatomic unsaturated N=N species can adsorb either end-on through one N or side-on through both N atoms. Since the current best came from a V-rich hollow, V appears highly favorable. To systematically search lower energy on a heterogeneous alloy surface, the next best distinct plan is to test a different V-rich coordination but not repeat the same V-V-V hollow. Among available sites, a Ta-V bridge is a strong chemically distinct alternative that still leverages V reactivity while probing alloy heterogeneity. Because the adsorbate has a radical center on atom 0, an end-on ReactiveSpecies plan through atom 0 is appropriate and respects the rules. This is completely different from the prior hollow V-V-V plan.

plan.reasoning: No prior attempts exist, so I should propose the best physically motivated initial adsorption plan rather than terminate. SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=N, i.e. diazenyl/nitrogen dimer representation with two heavy atoms. The provided atom list must be followed exactly: atom 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; atom 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. There is no mismatch between the requested adsorbate and the SMILES, so I continue based on the SMILES. Because one binding atom candidate (index 0) has an unpaired electron, ReactiveSpecies classification is chemically justified if I bind through that atom. For heterogeneous early-transition-metal-rich surfaces containing V and Ta, N-containing species typically bind most strongly at metal-rich multicoordinate sites rather than Si sites. Since the goal is lowest energy and there is no history yet, the most promising first guess is a hollow site composed only of transition metals, maximizing coordination and metal-nitrogen interaction. Among available hollow-3 options, V-V-V is expected to be strongest due to the more nitrophilic/reactive character of V compared with Si and often stronger than mixed sites; it is also chemically more plausible than involving Si for strong N adsorption. To preserve the N=N unit initially while allowing strong interaction, I choose single-point binding through the radical-bearing terminal N atom (index 0) at a hollow site. This satisfies the site alignment rules because hollow may use 1 binding index. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for [N]=N on this alloy surface, so this is an efficient high-priority first trial.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=N, i.e. diazenyl-like N2 with two heavy atoms. The provided atom list is authoritative: index 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; index 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. There is no mismatch between request and SMILES to warn about. Because I plan to bind through atom 0, which has one unpaired electron, classifying this as ReactiveSpecies is chemically consistent. Old plans analyzed: (1) hollow on V-V-V gave E = -6.585 eV after slipping to an HCP/subsurface-associated hollow; (2) bridge on Ta-V gave a much lower E = -7.855 eV, but it also relaxed into a hollow-like HCP/subsurface environment with a Ta,Ta,V,V,V,V local fingerprint, indicating a very favorable mixed Ta/V ensemble. There is no [🔄 Converged to known best] tag, so termination is not required. We also should not terminate yet because only two initial motifs have been tested, and the best result strongly suggests chemically distinct hollow environments on this heterogeneous V/Ta/Si surface. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration of [N]=N on the alloy surface. For this adsorbate, the most relevant adsorption motifs are end-on through the radical N or side-on through the N=N unit. Since the best current structure emerged from a Ta-V bridge slipping into a mixed hollow, a directly targeted mixed hollow is the most systematic next step. Among available hollows, Ta-V-V is a natural candidate because the current best local environment is V-rich but includes Ta; this may better access the favorable mixed ensemble than V-V-V while remaining distinct from the already tested Ta-V bridge and V-V-V hollow. To keep the plan new relative to history, I will use a hollow site with surface binding atoms ['Ta','V','V']. Because this is a radical adsorbate and only atom 0 has an unpaired electron, I will use a single binding index [0], which is allowed for hollow adsorption under the stated rules.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=N, a diatomic N2-like/open-shell species. The provided atom list has two nitrogens: index 0 is SP2 with 1 radical electron, index 1 is SP2 with 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch between the request and the SMILES because the user only asked for [N]=N adsorption. Because I plan to use the radical-bearing N atom as the binding center, ReactiveSpecies is chemically justified and the chosen binding atom indeed has unpaired electrons. History analysis: three successful plans have already been tested. hollow on V-V-V gave -6.585 eV. bridge on Ta-V slipped into a hollow-like HCP/subsurface environment and gave the current best energy of -7.855 eV. hollow on Ta-V-V gave -7.713 eV. No failures involving dissociation or bond changes are reported. There is no [🔄 Converged to known best] tag, so termination is not yet mandatory. The tested results suggest Ta-containing mixed V environments are substantially better than pure V hollow, and the best state appears associated with a Ta-rich/mixed hollow-like pocket reached from a Ta-V bridge. Since this alloy surface is heterogeneous, a chemically distinct Ta-containing site could still outperform the current best. To remain completely different from previous plans, I should not reuse hollow Ta-V-V, hollow V-V-V, or bridge Ta-V. A reasonable unexplored high-symmetry option is a Ta-Ta-V hollow, which is available and probes a more Ta-enriched threefold ensemble than Ta-V-V. User intent: find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for [N]=N on this surface. Adsorbate analysis: diatomic unsaturated N=N species; likely binds through one N end in an end-on mode or potentially side-on, but the radical localization on atom 0 makes end-on adsorption via atom 0 the most defensible next trial. Surface analysis: allowed sites include hollow Ta-Ta-V, which has not been tested and is chemically promising given the favorable Ta-containing history. Plan: use hollow site with surface atoms ['Ta','Ta','V'] and adsorbate binding index [0]. This satisfies the site rules because hollow may use 1 binding index.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=N, i.e. diazenyl-like N2 with two heavy atoms. RDKit indices are fixed as N(0) and N(1), both SP2; atom 0 has 1 radical electron and atom 1 has 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch between the requested adsorbate and the SMILES. Because one binding atom with an unpaired electron is available, using ReactiveSpecies is chemically valid if binding through atom 0. History analysis: four successful plans have already been tested. hollow on V-V-V gave -6.585 eV. bridge on Ta-V slipped to a hollow HCP/subsurface environment and is the current best at -7.855 eV. hollow on Ta-V-V gave -7.713 eV. hollow on Ta-Ta-V gave -7.564 eV. No failures with bond changes are reported, and there is no [🔄 Converged to known best] tag, so termination is not yet required. The tested low-energy environments are all V-rich hollows, with the best one emerging from a Ta-V bridge initialization, which suggests local alloy heterogeneity near Ta/V ensembles is important. To systematically search for a lower energy configuration, the next untested high-symmetry site that is chemically plausible is a V-V bridge. This can probe whether side-on/end-on placement over a pure V pair accesses an even better V-rich hollow/fourfold-like minimum after relaxation, distinct from the already tested Ta-V bridge and the hollow starts. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration on this mixed V/Ta/Si surface, so we should continue exploring an untested major site rather than terminate. Adsorbate analysis: [N]=N can adsorb end-on through the radical N atom most safely under ReactiveSpecies rules; side-on two-point adsorption would require both chosen atoms to satisfy the radical requirement, which atom 1 does not. Therefore I will use only adsorbate index 0. Surface analysis: V-V bridge exists in the allowed site list and has not yet been tried. This is a completely new plan relative to all previous attempts.
